title: Candidate #2 Fails Statistical Validation (Retraction of 026)

content.finding: Candidate #2 (Session 7 breakthrough) fails statistical validation with 10 seeds. Training: p=0.295 (d=0.35), Novel: p=0.972 (d=0.02). The +7.6% training / +37.6% novel gains were seed-specific, not generalizable.
content.evidence: 10-seed paired t-test: seeds [42,137,2024,7,314,1618,2718,3141,9999,31337]. Deltas range -0.063 to +0.186. Seeds 42, 2024, 314, 9999 show NEGATIVE deltas (Candidate #2 worse than canonical).
implications: 5-seed validation is insufficient — need 10+ seeds minimum
Parameter optimization may face flat landscape dominated by seed variance
content.key_insight: The plasticity rule parameter space may be dominated by seed-to-seed variance rather than parameter effects. Cohen's d=0.02 for novel signals means effectively zero effect size. Robust validation requires N>=10 seeds.

constraints_implied: 5-seed validation is insufficient for detecting parameter effects in this system
